findings.evidence: `rg` shows the only new theorem at `Covering.lean:45`; its body is just `rintro _ ⟨γ, rfl⟩ ... rw [Subsingleton.elim γ 1]`, while Mathlib provides the needed subsingleton instance for simply connected spaces at `mathlib/Mathlib/AlgebraicTopology/FundamentalGroupoid/SimplyConnected.lean:69`.
findings.fix: State/prove the general lemma first, e.g. `(FundamentalGroup.map f a₀).range = ⊥` or `∀ H, (FundamentalGroup.map f a₀).range ≤ H`, then derive the covering criterion instance by applying it to `(FundamentalGroup.mapOfEq p he).range` if that specialized spelling is still needed.
findings.issue: `IsCoveringMap.fundamentalGroupMap_range_le_of_simplyConnected` carries the covering-specific parameters `p e₀ he` even though the proof only uses `[SimplyConnectedSpace A]` and `f`; the natural statement is that `(FundamentalGroup.map f a₀).range` is trivial, or is contained in any subgroup of `FundamentalGroup X (f a₀)`.

summary: The remaining lemma is under-generalized: it proves a triviality of maps out of a simply connected fundamental group only for the specific covering-map subgroup.
